TextENGNew: ``...Hello?@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2002. Yes, it's me.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2003. How is our little Japanese Joan of Arc doing these days?``
``Hee hee... purely out of curiosity, that's all.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2005. There's nothing particularly frightening about her.``
``Things seem to be getting rather dramatic over on that end, but it doesn't appear to pose any obstacle to our research plans.``

``The police are ultimately just civil servants who can only exercise power within the confines of the law.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2008. They're nothing but a pack of lapdogs, heads bowed and tails wagging before the 'master' who controls them.``
changeReason: Old says 'civil servants can only exercise their power within the framework of the law' as generic; new frames police dismissively as 'ultimately just civil servants' and uses 'pack of lapdogs' vs 'group of people,' closer to the contemptuous JPN tone.

TextENGNew: ``...I'm rather curious to see how far she can get in that power game.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2010. Hee hee hee...``
``So... how are things at the moment?@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2012. Under normal circumstances, we should be seeing changes in behavior and appearance by now... Has anything changed?``

``...Hmm, is that so.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2014. No pronounced manic-depressive episodes observed, no changes in work performance.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2015. And her reputation among those around her remains favorable... I see.``
``How very intriguing.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2017. Could it be that Director Irie's improved medication was simply that compatible with her body?``

``Yes... that's right.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2019. As a precursor to reaching the terminal stage, suspicion toward one's surroundings intensifies markedly.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2020. An offhand joke or careless remark begins to feel like malice or hostility that negates one's very right to exist.``

``You might call it anxiety neurosis, I suppose.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2022. Eventually, the patient begins hallucinating things that don't exist, their thoughts become confused... and the psyche descends into utter chaos.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2023. At that point, neither reason nor instinct remains.``

``They simply spiral into bewilderment, desperately struggling to escape the tangled mess of reality and delusion, suffering, and finally collapsing into despair.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2025. When the terminal stage fully manifests... hee hee hee, it's quite a spectacle.``
``They are no longer human at that point... Yes, the most fitting word would be 'monster.'``
``...Oh?@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2028. Do I really sound like I'm enjoying this?``
``Hee hee... not at all.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2030. My interest is purely academic.``

``When an 'elite' who charged forward armed with naive idealism and a creed of pretty platitudes is forced to confront the ugly, filthy face of reality and despairs at her own powerlessness... how will she change?``
changeReason: Old says 'whitewashing and naive ideas of justice'; new says 'naive idealism and a creed of pretty platitudes' -- JPN 綺麗事だらけを並べ立てた信念 means 'beliefs built from nothing but pretty words.' Also new specifies 'she' where old was generic.

TextENGNew: ``You know what they say, don't you?@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2033. The higher the ideals someone studies with, the harder they fall when those beautiful dreams are crushed—turning to reckless violence or escapism.``
``This Joan of Arc... no, perhaps I should call her a female Don Quixote.``
``After she discovers that the demon she was fighting was nothing but a windmill, and that the justice she believed in was pitifully fragile... what will she do?``
``Perhaps the confusion will trigger terminal onset... and she'll rampage through the streets.``
``An elite police officer, driven mad by stress, massacring innocent citizens... Isn't that an ending just delicious to imagine?``
changeReason: Old translates 楽しそう as 'just fun to imagine'; new uses 'just delicious to imagine' -- 'delicious' conveys more sadistic relish matching Takano's character, though the literal JPN simply means 'seems fun.'

TextENGNew: ``Hee hee, ahahahahaha... aaahahahahahaha!!!``
``So with that said... do continue the surveillance, won't you?``
``Yes... yes, please give Nomura-san my regards as well.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2041. Goodbye, then.``
``......@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2043. Tomoe Minai... hm.``
...It was a strange feeling.
We have state power behind us—not as much as Uncle Koizumi had, but still.
There are no elements that could pose a meaningful threat to the execution of the plan.
changeReason: Old attributes 'She possesses no particular elements' about Minai specifically; new uses impersonal 'There are no elements' matching the JPN passive construction, shifting framing from personal weakness to general assessment.

TextENGNew: Even if the police united and that little girl put on the most spectacular heroic performance imaginable, penetrating to the heart of the organization would be difficult—no, impossible.
Besides, even in the unlikely event things took a dangerous turn... given the current power dynamics, it would be our rival faction—the Okuno-Chiba group—who would bear the brunt of the damage.
Not us.
So there should be no problem ignoring such movements entirely...
There shouldn't be anything to worry about at all, and yet...
My gaze drifted to the desk, where I had left out the medical file labeled... 'Tomoe Minai.'
In addition to the blood test data from when she came to Hinamizawa half a year ago, the file contained a comprehensive report on her personal background.
It detailed the loss of her parents in a fire, and her career from the police academy to the present day... and as I read it, a strange, disquieting sensation welled up within me.
No, that's not it.
This isn't unease.
It's the opposite, in fact... Yes—it's deja vu.

She lost her parents in an absurd 'accident,' was stripped of everything she had... and despite being hurled into the very depths of misery, she crawled her way back up to carry on the will of the deceased... with the stubborn tenacity of a weed.
...We're alike.
The parallels in our circumstances were infuriatingly precise... and the awareness of it only intensified that indescribable feeling churning inside me.
``...Hmph.@k@vS20/09/VTK2_takano2046. How absurd... truly absurd.``
Shaking my head, I tossed the file into the back of the desk drawer.
...Fine.
The outcome and the victor are already obvious. She can struggle all she likes until then.
But know this... little girl.
I commend you for the hardships you've overcome and for your indomitable will and courage—but remember this one thing clearly.
I will never acknowledge you.
Never...!!
